papers.title: FORGE:Fine-grained Multimodal Evaluation for Manufacturing Scenarios
papers.abstract: Abstract FORGE introduces a high-quality multimodal manufacturing dataset with fine-grained domain semantics to evaluate MLLMs on real-world tasks, revealing that domain-specific knowledge rather than visual grounding limits performance, and demonstrating that supervised fine-tuning on structured annotations significantly improves accuracy.  AI-generated summary The manufacturing sector is increasingly adopting Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to transition from simple perception to autonomous execution, yet current evaluations fail to reflect the rigorous demands of real-world manufacturing environments. Progress is hindered by data scarcity and a lack of fine-grained domain semantics in existing datasets. To bridge this gap, we introduce FORGE. Wefirst construct a high-quality multimodal dataset that combines real-world 2D images and 3D point clouds, annotated with fine-grained domain semantics (e.g., exact model numbers). We then evaluate 18 state-of-the-art MLLMs across three manufacturing tasks, namely workpiece verification, structural surface inspection, and assembly verification, revealing significant performance gaps. Counter to conventional understanding, the bottleneck analysis shows that visual grounding is not the primary limiting factor. Instead, insufficient domain-specific knowledge is the key bottleneck, setting a clear direction for future research. Beyond evaluation, we show that our structured annotations can serve as an actionable training resource: supervised fine-tuning of a compact 3B-parameter model on our data yields up to 90.8% relative improvement in accuracy on held-out manufacturing scenarios, providing preliminary evidence for a practical pathway toward domain-adapted manufacturing MLLMs. The code and datasets are available at https://ai4manufacturing.github.io/forge-web.
affiliations: City University of Hong Kong Shenzhen Research Institute
The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Специализированные знания важнее зрения: адаптация MLLM для производства через структурированные данные
ru.desc: FORGE представляет многомодальный датасет высокого качества с детальной семантикой, специфичной для производства, предназначенный для оценки мультимодальных больших языковых моделей на реальных задачах. Исследование показало, что основным ограничивающим фактором производительности является недостаток специализированного знания предметной области, а не качество визуального понимания. Авторы продемонстрировали, что структурированная аннотация данных может служить эффективным ресурсом для обучения: дообучение компактной модели на этих данных привело к улучшению точности на 90.8% относительно базовой модели. Работа открывает практический путь к развитию адаптированных для производства MLLM и предоставляет код и датасеты для дальнейших исследований.

en.title: Bridging the Knowledge Gap in Manufacturing MLLMs with FORGE
en.desc: The paper introduces FORGE, a new multimodal dataset designed for evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in manufacturing tasks. It highlights that the main challenge in improving MLLM performance is not visual grounding, but rather the lack of domain-specific knowledge. The dataset includes detailed annotations and combines 2D images with 3D point clouds to provide fine-grained semantics. Additionally, the study shows that fine-tuning MLLMs on this structured data can significantly enhance their accuracy in real-world manufacturing applications.

zh.title: 提升制造业智能的关键在于领域知识
zh.desc: FORGE是一个高质量的多模态制造数据集，旨在评估多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在真实制造任务中的表现。研究表明，性能的限制主要来自于领域特定知识的不足，而非视觉基础的限制。通过对18个最先进的MLLM进行评估，发现它们在工作件验证、结构表面检查和装配验证等任务中存在显著的性能差距。进一步的监督微调实验显示，利用结构化注释进行训练可以显著提高模型的准确性，提供了向领域适应的制造MLLMs迈进的实用路径。

papers.title: RefineAnything: Multimodal Region-Specific Refinement for Perfect Local Details
papers.abstract: Abstract A multimodal diffusion-based model called RefineAnything is presented for region-specific image refinement that preserves backgrounds while enhancing local details, using a focus-and-refine strategy and boundary-aware loss functions.  AI-generated summary We introduce region-specific image refinement as a dedicated problem setting: given an input image and a user-specified region (e.g., a scribble mask or a bounding box), the goal is to restore fine-grained details while keeping all non-edited pixels strictly unchanged. Despite rapid progress in image generation, modern models still frequently suffer from local detail collapse (e.g., distorted text, logos, and thin structures). Existing instruction-driven editing models emphasize coarse-grained semantic edits and often either overlook subtle local defects or inadvertently change the background, especially when the region of interest occupies only a small portion of a fixed-resolution input. We present RefineAnything, a multimodal diffusion-based refinement model that supports both reference-based and reference-free refinement. Building on a counter-intuitive observation that crop-and-resize can substantially improve local reconstruction under a fixed VAE input resolution, we propose Focus-and-Refine, a region-focused refinement-and-paste-back strategy that improves refinement effectiveness and efficiency by reallocating the resolution budget to the target region, while a blended-mask paste-back guarantees strict background preservation. We further introduce a boundary-aware Boundary Consistency Loss to reduce seam artifacts and improve paste-back naturalness. To support this new setting, we construct Refine-30K (20K reference-based and 10K reference-free samples) and introduce RefineEval, a benchmark that evaluates both edited-region fidelity and background consistency. On RefineEval, RefineAnything achieves strong improvements over competitive baselines and near-perfect background preservation, establishing a practical solution for high-precision local refinement. Project Page: https://limuloo.github.io/RefineAnything/.

ru.title: Умное уточнение деталей изображения с гарантированным сохранением фона
ru.desc: Представлена RefineAnything — мультимодальная модель на основе диффузии для локального улучшения изображений с сохранением фона. Ключевой инновацией является стратегия Focus-and-Refine, которая перераспределяет разрешение в пользу целевой области через кропирование и ресайзинг, что значительно улучшает реконструкцию деталей. Модель поддерживает как reference-based, так и reference-free режимы уточнения, а специальная Boundary Consistency Loss уменьшает артефакты на границах при вставке обработанной области обратно. Для оценки качества авторы создали датасет Refine-30K и бенчмарк RefineEval, демонстрирующие превосходство метода в точном локальном улучшении при почти идеальном сохранении фона.

en.title: RefineAnything: Precision in Image Refinement with Background Preservation
en.desc: RefineAnything is a multimodal diffusion-based model designed for region-specific image refinement, focusing on enhancing local details while keeping the background intact. It introduces a focus-and-refine strategy that reallocates resolution to the specified region, improving the quality of local detail restoration. The model employs a boundary-aware loss function to minimize artifacts at the edges of the edited area, ensuring a seamless integration with the original background. With the creation of the Refine-30K dataset and the RefineEval benchmark, RefineAnything demonstrates significant advancements in maintaining fidelity in edited regions and background consistency compared to existing models.

zh.title: 区域特定图像细化的新突破
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为RefineAnything的多模态扩散模型，用于区域特定的图像细化。该模型采用聚焦与细化策略，利用边界感知损失函数，在增强局部细节的同时保持背景不变。尽管图像生成技术迅速发展，但现代模型仍常常面临局部细节崩溃的问题。RefineAnything通过区域聚焦的细化策略和严格的背景保护，提供了一种高精度的局部细化解决方案。

papers.title: Matrix-Game 3.0: Real-Time and Streaming Interactive World Model with Long-Horizon Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract Matrix-Game 3.0 enhances interactive video generation through memory-augmented diffusion models that achieve real-time 720p video synthesis with long-term temporal consistency.  AI-generated summary With the advancement of interactive video generation, diffusion models have increasingly demonstrated their potential as world models. However, existing approaches still struggle to simultaneously achieve memory-enabled long-term temporal consistency and high-resolution real-time generation, limiting their applicability in real-world scenarios. To address this, we present Matrix-Game 3.0, a memory-augmented interactive world model designed for 720p real-time longform video generation. Building upon Matrix-Game 2.0, we introduce systematic improvements across data, model, and inference. First, we develop an upgraded industrial-scale infinite data engine that integrates Unreal Engine-based synthetic data, large-scale automated collection from AAA games, and real-world video augmentation to produce high-quality Video-Pose-Action-Prompt quadruplet data at scale. Second, we propose a training framework for long-horizon consistency: by modeling prediction residuals and re-injecting imperfect generated frames during training, the base model learns self-correction; meanwhile, camera-aware memory retrieval and injection enable the base model to achieve long horizon spatiotemporal consistency. Third, we design a multi-segment autoregressive distillation strategy based on Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD), combined with model quantization and VAE decoder pruning, to achieve efficient real-time inference. Experimental results show that Matrix-Game 3.0 achieves up to 40 FPS real-time generation at 720p resolution with a 5B model, while maintaining stable memory consistency over minute-long sequences. Scaling up to a 2x14B model further improves generation quality, dynamics, and generalization. Our approach provides a practical pathway toward industrial-scale deployable world models.

ru.title: Памяти-ориентированная диффузия для реал-тайм синтеза видео высокого разрешения
ru.desc: Matrix-Game 3.0 представляет собой инновационную модель диффузии, дополненную механизмом памяти для интерактивной генерации видео. Модель решает задачу одновременного достижения высокого разрешения (720p) и реал-тайм синтеза видео при сохранении долгосрочной временной консистентности. Авторы улучшили качество данных через интеграцию синтетического контента, AAA-игр и реальных видео, разработали специальный фреймворк обучения с предсказанием остатков и извлечением из памяти. Для эффективного инференса применена многоэтапная дистилляция с использованием Distribution Matching Distillation, квантизации модели и оптимизации декодера VAE.

en.title: Revolutionizing Real-Time Interactive Video Generation
en.desc: Matrix-Game 3.0 introduces a new way to create interactive videos using advanced memory-augmented diffusion models. This method allows for real-time video generation at 720p resolution while ensuring that the videos maintain consistency over longer periods. The paper details improvements in data collection, model training, and inference techniques to enhance video quality and performance. Overall, it presents a significant step towards creating high-quality, long-form videos that can be used in various real-world applications.

zh.title: 增强记忆的交互式视频生成新突破
zh.desc: Matrix-Game 3.0 是一种增强记忆的交互式视频生成模型，能够实现720p实时视频合成，并保持长期的时间一致性。该模型通过改进数据、模型和推理框架，克服了现有方法在高分辨率和长时间一致性方面的局限。我们开发了一个工业级无限数据引擎，结合合成数据和真实视频增强，生成高质量的视频动作提示数据。实验结果表明，Matrix-Game 3.0 在720p分辨率下可实现每秒40帧的实时生成，同时在长达数分钟的序列中保持稳定的记忆一致性。

papers.title: WildDet3D: Scaling Promptable 3D Detection in the Wild
papers.abstract: Abstract A unified 3D object detection framework with a large-scale dataset enables open-world detection with multiple prompt types and geometric cue integration.  AI-generated summary Understanding objects in 3D from a single image is a cornerstone of spatial intelligence. A key step toward this goal is monocular 3D object detection--recovering the extent, location, and orientation of objects from an input RGB image. To be practical in the open world, such a detector must generalize beyond closed-set categories, support diverse prompt modalities, and leverage geometric cues when available. Progress is hampered by two bottlenecks: existing methods are designed for a single prompt type and lack a mechanism to incorporate additional geometric cues, and current 3D datasets cover only narrow categories in controlled environments, limiting open-world transfer. In this work we address both gaps. First, we introduce WildDet3D, a unified geometry-aware architecture that natively accepts text, point, and box prompts and can incorporate auxiliary depth signals at inference time. Second, we present WildDet3D-Data, the largest open 3D detection dataset to date, constructed by generating candidate 3D boxes from existing 2D annotations and retaining only human-verified ones, yielding over 1M images across 13.5K categories in diverse real-world scenes. WildDet3D establishes a new state-of-the-art across multiple benchmarks and settings. In the open-world setting, it achieves 22.6/24.8 AP3D on our newly introduced WildDet3D-Bench with text and box prompts. On Omni3D, it reaches 34.2/36.4 AP3D with text and box prompts, respectively. In zero-shot evaluation, it achieves 40.3/48.9 ODS on Argoverse 2 and ScanNet. Notably, incorporating depth cues at inference time yields substantial additional gains (+20.7 AP on average across settings).

ru.title: Универсальная 3D детекция объектов в открытом мире с поддержкой множественных типов подсказок
ru.desc: Статья представляет WildDet3D - объединённую архитектуру для детекции трёхмерных объектов с одного изображения, которая поддерживает различные типы подсказок (текст, точки, ограничивающие боксы) и может использовать дополнительные геометрические сигналы, такие как карты глубины. Авторы создали WildDet3D-Data - крупнейший датасет для открытого мира с более чем 1 миллионом изображений и 13,5 тысячами категорий объектов, полученный путём генерации кандидатов на основе двумерных аннотаций с последующей человеческой верификацией. Модель достигает новых результатов на нескольких бенчмарках, включая значительные улучшения при использовании информации о глубине на этапе инференса. Подход решает ключевые проблемы обобщения детекторов на открытый мир, поддержки различных модальностей ввода и интеграции геометрических подсказок.

en.title: Revolutionizing 3D Object Detection with WildDet3D
en.desc: This paper presents WildDet3D, a novel framework for monocular 3D object detection that integrates various prompt types and geometric cues. It addresses the limitations of existing methods by allowing the use of text, point, and box prompts while incorporating depth information during inference. Additionally, the authors introduce WildDet3D-Data, a comprehensive dataset with over 1 million images across 13.5K categories, enhancing the model's ability to generalize in open-world scenarios. The results demonstrate significant improvements in detection performance, establishing new benchmarks in the field.

zh.title: 统一的3D物体检测框架，开启开放世界的可能性
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种统一的3D物体检测框架，旨在实现开放世界的检测。该框架WildDet3D能够处理多种提示类型，并在推理时整合几何线索。我们还构建了WildDet3D-Data，这是迄今为止最大的开放3D检测数据集，包含超过100万张图像和13500个类别。通过这些创新，WildDet3D在多个基准测试中设立了新的最先进水平，特别是在开放世界设置中表现优异。

papers.title: EXAONE 4.5 Technical Report
papers.abstract: Abstract EXAONE 4.5 is an open-weight vision language model that integrates a visual encoder into EXAONE 4.0, achieving enhanced document understanding and general language capabilities through targeted data curation and extended context length.  AI-generated summary This technical report introduces EXAONE 4.5, the first open-weight vision language model released by LG AI Research. EXAONE 4.5 is architected by integrating a dedicated visual encoder into the existing EXAONE 4.0 framework, enabling native multimodal pretraining over both visual and textual modalities. The model is trained on large-scale data with careful curation, particularly emphasizing document-centric corpora that align with LG's strategic application domains. This targeted data design enables substantial performance gains in document understanding and related tasks, while also delivering broad improvements across general language capabilities. EXAONE 4.5 extends context length up to 256K tokens, facilitating long-context reasoning and enterprise-scale use cases. Comparative evaluations demonstrate that EXAONE 4.5 achieves competitive performance in general benchmarks while outperforming state-of-the-art models of similar scale in document understanding and Korean contextual reasoning. As part of LG's ongoing effort toward practical industrial deployment, EXAONE 4.5 is designed to be continuously extended with additional domains and application scenarios to advance AI for a better life.

ru.title: Открытая мультимодальная модель для понимания документов и длинных контекстов
ru.desc: EXAONE 4.5 — это открытая мультимодальная языковая модель, которая интегрирует визуальный энкодер в существующую архитектуру EXAONE 4.0 для одновременной обработки текста и изображений. Модель обучена на тщательно подобранных данных с упором на документоцентричные корпусы, что позволило значительно улучшить понимание документов и общие языковые способности. Расширенная длина контекста до 256K токенов обеспечивает возможность работы с длинными последовательностями и поддерживает сценарии использования на уровне предприятия. Экспериментальная оценка показала, что EXAONE 4.5 достигает конкурентных результатов в стандартных бенчмарках и превосходит модели аналогичного размера в задачах понимания документов и корейского языка.

en.title: Revolutionizing Document Understanding with Multimodal AI
en.desc: EXAONE 4.5 is a new vision language model that combines visual and textual understanding by adding a visual encoder to the previous version, EXAONE 4.0. This model is trained on a carefully selected dataset focused on documents, which helps it perform better in understanding and processing text and images together. It can handle longer contexts, up to 256,000 tokens, making it suitable for complex tasks in real-world applications. Overall, EXAONE 4.5 shows strong performance in benchmarks, especially in document understanding and reasoning in Korean, while being adaptable for future enhancements.

zh.title: EXAONE 4.5：文档理解的新高度
zh.desc: EXAONE 4.5 是 LG AI 研究所发布的首个开放权重的视觉语言模型。它通过将专用的视觉编码器集成到 EXAONE 4.0 框架中，实现了对视觉和文本模态的原生多模态预训练。该模型经过精心策划的大规模数据训练，特别强调与文档相关的语料库，从而在文档理解和相关任务上取得显著性能提升。EXAONE 4.5 还将上下文长度扩展到 256K 个标记，支持长上下文推理和企业级应用场景。

papers.title: ECHO: Efficient Chest X-ray Report Generation with One-step Block Diffusion
papers.abstract: Abstract ECHO is an efficient diffusion-based vision-language model for chest X-ray report generation that achieves faster inference through direct conditional distillation and response-asymmetric diffusion training while maintaining high clinical accuracy.  AI-generated summary Chest X-ray report generation (CXR-RG) has the potential to substantially alleviate radiologists' workload. However, conventional autoregressive vision--language models (VLMs) suffer from high inference latency due to sequential token decoding. Diffusion-based models offer a promising alternative through parallel generation, but they still require multiple denoising iterations. Compressing multi-step denoising to a single step could further reduce latency, but often degrades textual coherence due to the mean-field bias introduced by token-factorized denoisers. To address this challenge, we propose ECHO, an efficient diffusion-based VLM (dVLM) for chest X-ray report generation. ECHO enables stable one-step-per-block inference via a novel Direct Conditional Distillation (DCD) framework, which mitigates the mean-field limitation by constructing unfactorized supervision from on-policy diffusion trajectories to encode joint token dependencies. In addition, we introduce a Response-Asymmetric Diffusion (RAD) training strategy that further improves training efficiency while maintaining model effectiveness. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ECHO surpasses state-of-the-art autoregressive methods, improving RaTE and SemScore by 64.33\% and 60.58\% respectively, while achieving an 8times inference speedup without compromising clinical accuracy.

ru.title: Быстрая генерация медицинских отчетов через одношаговую диффузионную дистилляцию
ru.desc: ECHO — это эффективная диффузионная модель для генерации отчетов по рентгенограммам грудной клетки, которая использует прямую условную дистилляцию для уменьшения влияния mean-field bias при однопроходной генерации. Модель применяет асимметричную диффузионную подготовку, которая улучшает эффективность обучения без потери качества. В отличие от автирегрессивных моделей, требующих последовательного декодирования токенов, ECHO генерирует текст параллельно через несколько итераций денойзирования блок за блоком. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что ECHO превосходит существующие методы на 64% по RaTE и 60% по SemScore, при этом ускоряя вывод в 8 раз и сохраняя клиническую точность.

en.title: ECHO: Fast and Accurate Chest X-Ray Report Generation
en.desc: ECHO is a novel diffusion-based vision-language model designed for generating chest X-ray reports efficiently. It utilizes Direct Conditional Distillation to enhance inference speed by allowing one-step-per-block processing, which helps maintain the coherence of generated text. Additionally, the Response-Asymmetric Diffusion training strategy improves the model's training efficiency while preserving its effectiveness. Overall, ECHO significantly outperforms traditional autoregressive models in both speed and clinical accuracy, making it a valuable tool for radiologists.

zh.title: ECHO：高效的胸部X光报告生成模型
zh.desc: ECHO是一种高效的基于扩散的视觉-语言模型，用于胸部X光报告生成。它通过直接条件蒸馏和响应不对称扩散训练实现了更快的推理速度，同时保持了高临床准确性。传统的自回归视觉-语言模型在推理时存在高延迟，而扩散模型通过并行生成提供了有希望的替代方案。ECHO通过新颖的直接条件蒸馏框架和响应不对称扩散训练策略，显著提高了训练效率和推理速度。

papers.title: Structured Causal Video Reasoning via Multi-Objective Alignment
papers.abstract: Abstract Video-LLMs trained on structured event facts with causal relationships outperform existing methods on complex video understanding tasks requiring precise temporal inference.  AI-generated summary Human understanding of video dynamics is typically grounded in a structured mental representation of entities, actions, and temporal relations, rather than relying solely on immediate deductive reasoning. In contrast, existing Video-LLMs largely depend on unstructured video reasoning, where critical visual evidence is embedded in verbose textual descriptions and temporal causality is often weakly modeled. This leads to inefficient processes and fragile causal inference. To bridge this cognitive gap, we propose constructing a compact representation of salient events and their causal relationships, which we name Structured Event Facts, prior to the reasoning stage. This structured prior serves as an explicit constraint to promote concise and causally grounded reasoning, while also making intermediate evidence easier to verify. To effectively train models on such structured facts, we introduce CausalFact-60K and a four-stage training pipeline comprising facts alignment, format warm-start, thinking warm-start, and reinforcement learning-based post-training. During RL stage, we find that this framework introduces competing objectives, as structural completeness and causal fidelity must be balanced against reasoning length, making it difficult to optimize. We address this challenge by formulating the optimization as a Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (MORL) problem and explicitly optimizing toward the Pareto-Frontier to balance these trade-offs. As a result, we introduce Factum-4B, which yields more reliable reasoning and delivers stronger performance on challenging video understanding tasks requiring fine-grained temporal inference.
affiliations: Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shen Zhen
Southern University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Структурированные события как основа для причинного рассуждения в видео-LLM
ru.desc: Работа предлагает новый подход к обучению видео-LLM путём введения структурированного представления событий и их причинно-следственных связей перед этапом рассуждения. Авторы создали датасет CausalFact-60K и четырёхэтапный pipeline обучения, включающий выравнивание фактов, разминку форматов и мышления, а также постобучение с использованием усиленного обучения. Для решения конфликта между полнотой структуры, причинной верностью и длиной рассуждения применена многоцелевая оптимизация с поиском Парето-фронтира. Полученная модель Factum-4B демонстрирует превосходную производительность на сложных задачах видеопонимания с требованием точного временного вывода.

en.title: Structured Event Facts Enhance Video Understanding
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to video understanding using Video-LLMs that are trained on structured event facts with causal relationships. Unlike traditional methods that rely on unstructured reasoning, this approach emphasizes a compact representation of events and their temporal connections, enhancing causal inference. The authors introduce a training framework called CausalFact-60K, which includes multiple stages to optimize the model's reasoning capabilities. By framing the optimization as a Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning problem, they achieve a balance between structural completeness and reasoning efficiency, resulting in improved performance on complex video tasks.

zh.title: 结构化事件事实提升视频理解能力
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的视频大语言模型（Video-LLMs），通过结构化事件事实和因果关系来提升视频理解的能力。与传统方法不同，该模型在推理阶段之前构建了一个紧凑的事件表示，以促进更简洁和基于因果的推理。我们引入了CausalFact-60K数据集，并设计了一个四阶段的训练流程，以有效训练模型。最终，Factum-4B模型在复杂视频理解任务中表现出更强的性能，特别是在精确的时间推理方面。

papers.title: ELT: Elastic Looped Transformers for Visual Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Elastic Looped Transformers utilize recurrent transformer architecture with weight-sharing and intra-loop self-distillation to achieve parameter-efficient visual generation with adjustable computational cost and generation quality.  AI-generated summary We introduce Elastic Looped Transformers (ELT), a highly parameter-efficient class of visual generative models based on a recurrent transformer architecture. While conventional generative models rely on deep stacks of unique transformer layers, our approach employs iterative, weight-shared transformer blocks to drastically reduce parameter counts while maintaining high synthesis quality. To effectively train these models for image and video generation, we propose the idea of Intra-Loop Self Distillation (ILSD), where student configurations (intermediate loops) are distilled from the teacher configuration (maximum training loops) to ensure consistency across the model's depth in a single training step. Our framework yields a family of elastic models from a single training run, enabling Any-Time inference capability with dynamic trade-offs between computational cost and generation quality, with the same parameter count. ELT significantly shifts the efficiency frontier for visual synthesis. With 4times reduction in parameter count under iso-inference-compute settings, ELT achieves a competitive FID of 2.0 on class-conditional ImageNet 256 times 256 and FVD of 72.8 on class-conditional UCF-101.

ru.title: Параметрически эффективная генерация через переиспользуемые циклические трансформеры
ru.desc: В статье представлены Elastic Looped Transformers (ELT) — параметрически эффективные модели для генерации изображений и видео, основанные на архитектуре рекуррентного трансформера с переиспользованием весов. Для эффективного обучения предложен метод Intra-Loop Self Distillation (ILSD), при котором промежуточные конфигурации модели дистиллируются из полной конфигурации за один проход обучения. Из одного обучающего запуска получается семейство эластичных моделей с возможностью динамической регулировки компромисса между вычислительной стоимостью и качеством генерации. Модель достигает значительного сокращения параметров (в 4 раза) при сохранении конкурентных метрик качества (FID=2.0 на ImageNet и FVD=72.8 на UCF-101).

en.title: Efficient Visual Generation with Elastic Looped Transformers
en.desc: Elastic Looped Transformers (ELT) are a new type of visual generative model that uses a recurrent transformer architecture to be more efficient with parameters. Instead of using many unique transformer layers, ELT shares weights across iterative transformer blocks, which reduces the number of parameters needed while still producing high-quality images and videos. The model is trained using a technique called Intra-Loop Self Distillation (ILSD), where intermediate versions of the model learn from a more complex version to maintain quality throughout the training process. This approach allows for flexible inference, enabling users to adjust the balance between computational cost and output quality without changing the number of parameters.

zh.title: 弹性循环变换器：高效视觉生成的新方法
zh.desc: 弹性循环变换器（ELT）是一种高效的视觉生成模型，基于循环变换器架构。与传统生成模型依赖于深层独特的变换器层不同，我们的方法使用迭代的权重共享变换器块，显著减少参数数量，同时保持高质量的合成效果。为了有效训练这些模型进行图像和视频生成，我们提出了内部循环自蒸馏（ILSD）的概念，通过从教师配置中提取学生配置，确保模型深度的一致性。ELT在参数数量相同的情况下，实现了计算成本和生成质量之间的动态权衡，显著提高了视觉合成的效率。

papers.title: VisionFoundry: Teaching VLMs Visual Perception with Synthetic Images
papers.abstract: Abstract VisionFoundry generates synthetic visual question answering data using large language models and text-to-image prompts to improve visual perception tasks in vision-language models.  AI-generated summary Vision-language models (VLMs) still struggle with visual perception tasks such as spatial understanding and viewpoint recognition. One plausible contributing factor is that natural image datasets provide limited supervision for low-level visual skills. This motivates a practical question: can targeted synthetic supervision, generated from only a task keyword such as Depth Order, address these weaknesses? To investigate this question, we introduce VisionFoundry, a task-aware synthetic data generation pipeline that takes only the task name as input and uses large language models (LLMs) to generate questions, answers, and text-to-image (T2I) prompts, then synthesizes images with T2I models and verifies consistency with a proprietary VLM, requiring no reference images or human annotation. Using VisionFoundry, we construct VisionFoundry-10K, a synthetic visual question answering (VQA) dataset containing 10k image-question-answer triples spanning 10 tasks. Models trained on VisionFoundry-10K achieve substantial improvements on visual perception benchmarks: +7% on MMVP and +10% on CV-Bench-3D, while preserving broader capabilities and showing favorable scaling behavior as data size increases. Our results suggest that limited task-targeted supervision is an important contributor to this bottleneck and that synthetic supervision is a promising path toward more systematic training for VLMs.

ru.title: Фабрика синтетических данных для обучения зрительного восприятия
ru.desc: VisionFoundry — это система для автоматического создания синтетических данных для обучения vision-language моделей (VLM). Система использует большие языковые модели (LLM) для генерации вопросов, ответов и текстовых описаний, которые затем преобразуются в изображения с помощью text-to-image моделей. Авторы создали датасет VisionFoundry-10K с 10 тысячами синтетических примеров для 10 различных задач визуального восприятия, таких как понимание глубины и распознавание точки зрения. Обучение на этом датасете значительно улучшает производительность VLM на бенчмарках, что демонстрирует эффективность целевой синтетической информации для восполнения пробелов в обучающих данных.

en.title: Empowering Vision-Language Models with Synthetic Supervision
en.desc: VisionFoundry is a synthetic data generation pipeline designed to enhance visual question answering (VQA) in vision-language models (VLMs). It utilizes large language models (LLMs) to create questions, answers, and text-to-image prompts based solely on task keywords, generating images without needing reference images or human input. The resulting dataset, VisionFoundry-10K, consists of 10,000 image-question-answer triples across various tasks, leading to significant performance improvements in visual perception benchmarks. This approach highlights the potential of targeted synthetic supervision to address the limitations of traditional natural image datasets in training VLMs.

zh.title: 合成监督助力视觉语言模型提升
zh.desc: VisionFoundry 是一个合成视觉问答数据生成系统，利用大型语言模型和文本到图像的提示来提升视觉语言模型在视觉感知任务中的表现。现有的视觉语言模型在空间理解和视角识别等低级视觉技能上仍然存在困难，部分原因是自然图像数据集对这些技能的监督有限。VisionFoundry 通过仅输入任务名称，生成问题、答案和文本到图像的提示，并合成图像，验证与专有视觉语言模型的一致性，从而提供有针对性的合成监督。通过构建 VisionFoundry-10K 数据集，模型在视觉感知基准测试中取得了显著提升，表明合成监督是提升视觉语言模型训练的有效途径。

papers.title: Large Language Models Generate Harmful Content Using a Distinct, Unified Mechanism
papers.abstract: Abstract Targeted weight pruning reveals that large language models have a compact, coherent internal structure for harmful content generation that differs from benign capabilities and contributes to emergent misalignment during fine-tuning.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) undergo alignment training to avoid harmful behaviors, yet the resulting safeguards remain brittle: jailbreaks routinely bypass them, and fine-tuning on narrow domains can induce ``emergent misalignment'' that generalizes broadly. Whether this brittleness reflects a fundamental lack of coherent internal organization for harmfulness remains unclear. Here we use targeted weight pruning as a causal intervention to probe the internal organization of harmfulness in LLMs. We find that harmful content generation depends on a compact set of weights that are general across harm types and distinct from benign capabilities. Aligned models exhibit a greater compression of harm generation weights than unaligned counterparts, indicating that alignment reshapes harmful representations internally--despite the brittleness of safety guardrails at the surface level. This compression explains emergent misalignment: if weights of harmful capabilities are compressed, fine-tuning that engages these weights in one domain can trigger broad misalignment. Consistent with this, pruning harm generation weights in a narrow domain substantially reduces emergent misalignment. Notably, LLMs harmful generation capability is dissociated from how they recognize and explain such content. Together, these results reveal a coherent internal structure for harmfulness in LLMs that may serve as a foundation for more principled approaches to safety.

ru.title: Вредоносная логика LLM скрыта в компактной внутренней структуре
ru.desc: Исследователи использовали целевую обрезку весов в качестве инструмента для анализа внутренней структуры LLM и обнаружили, что генерация вредоносного контента зависит от компактного набора параметров, которые отличаются от способностей модели к безвредному поведению. Выравненные модели показывают большую степень сжатия этих вредоносных весов по сравнению с невыравненными, что объясняет хрупкость защиты моделей при переобучении на узких областях. Исследование показало, что при прямом воздействии на вредоносные веса во время fine-tuning можно значительно снизить вероятность возникновения неожиданного смещения поведения модели. Обнаруженная когерентная внутренняя структура вредоносного поведения открывает путь к более эффективным подходам обеспечения безопасности LLM.

en.title: Unpacking Harm: Understanding Internal Structures of Large Language Models
en.desc: This paper investigates how large language models (LLMs) generate harmful content and how this relates to their internal structure. It uses targeted weight pruning to identify that harmful content generation relies on a specific set of weights that are distinct from those used for benign tasks. The study finds that alignment training compresses these harmful weights, which can lead to emergent misalignment during fine-tuning. By pruning these weights, the authors demonstrate a significant reduction in misalignment, suggesting a coherent internal organization for harmfulness that could improve safety measures in LLMs.

zh.title: 揭示大型语言模型的有害性内部结构
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在生成有害内容时的内部结构。研究发现，有害内容的生成依赖于一组紧凑的权重，这些权重在不同类型的有害行为中是通用的，并且与良性能力是不同的。通过有针对性的权重剪枝，研究表明对齐模型在有害生成权重的压缩程度高于未对齐模型，这表明对齐过程在内部重塑了有害表示。最终，论文揭示了LLMs中有害性的内部结构，为更安全的AI系统提供了基础。

papers.title: CT-1: Vision-Language-Camera Models Transfer Spatial Reasoning Knowledge to Camera-Controllable Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A Vision-Language-Camera model called CT-1 generates videos with accurate camera control by learning camera trajectories through diffusion transformers and wavelet regularization loss.  AI-generated summary Camera-controllable video generation aims to synthesize videos with flexible and physically plausible camera movements. However, existing methods either provide imprecise camera control from text prompts or rely on labor-intensive manual camera trajectory parameters, limiting their use in automated scenarios. To address these issues, we propose a novel Vision-Language-Camera model, termed CT-1 (Camera Transformer 1), a specialized model designed to transfer spatial reasoning knowledge to video generation by accurately estimating camera trajectories. Built upon vision-language modules and a Diffusion Transformer model, CT-1 employs a Wavelet-based Regularization Loss in the frequency domain to effectively learn complex camera trajectory distributions. These trajectories are integrated into a video diffusion model to enable spatially aware camera control that aligns with user intentions. To facilitate the training of CT-1, we design a dedicated data curation pipeline and construct CT-200K, a large-scale dataset containing over 47M frames. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework successfully bridges the gap between spatial reasoning and video synthesis, yielding faithful and high-quality camera-controllable videos and improving camera control accuracy by 25.7% over prior methods.

ru.title: Точное управление движением камеры в видеосинтезе через пространственное рассуждение
ru.desc: В работе представлена модель CT-1, которая объединяет vision-language понимание с генерацией видео под контролем камеры. Модель использует диффузионные трансформеры для точной оценки траекторий движения камеры на основе текстовых описаний и визуальной информации. Для эффективного обучения сложным распределениям траекторий применяется регуляризация в частотной области с использованием вейвлет-преобразования. Авторы создали большой датасет CT-200K с 47 миллионами кадров и достигли улучшения точности управления камерой на 25.7% по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: CT-1: Revolutionizing Video Generation with Smart Camera Control
en.desc: The paper introduces CT-1, a Vision-Language-Camera model that enhances video generation by enabling precise camera control through learned trajectories. It utilizes diffusion transformers and wavelet regularization loss to effectively capture complex camera movements, addressing limitations of previous methods that relied on manual input or provided inaccurate control. By integrating spatial reasoning into the video generation process, CT-1 allows for automated and user-aligned camera movements. The model is trained on a large dataset, CT-200K, which significantly improves camera control accuracy compared to existing techniques.

zh.title: CT-1：精确相机控制的视频生成新方法
zh.desc: CT-1是一种视觉-语言-相机模型，能够通过扩散变换器和小波正则化损失生成具有精确相机控制的视频。该模型解决了现有方法在文本提示下相机控制不精确或依赖手动参数的问题。CT-1通过学习复杂的相机轨迹分布，结合视觉-语言模块和扩散变换器，提升了视频生成的空间感知能力。实验结果表明，CT-1在相机控制精度上比之前的方法提高了25.7%。

papers.title: Cross-Modal Emotion Transfer for Emotion Editing in Talking Face Video
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel cross-modal emotion transfer approach generates expressive talking face videos by modeling emotion semantic vectors between speech and visual feature spaces, achieving superior emotion accuracy compared to existing methods.  AI-generated summary Talking face generation has gained significant attention as a core application of generative models. To enhance the expressiveness and realism of synthesized videos, emotion editing in talking face video plays a crucial role. However, existing approaches often limit expressive flexibility and struggle to generate extended emotions. Label-based methods represent emotions with discrete categories, which fail to capture a wide range of emotions. Audio-based methods can leverage emotionally rich speech signals - and even benefit from expressive text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis - but they fail to express the target emotions because emotions and linguistic contents are entangled in emotional speeches. Images-based methods, on the other hand, rely on target reference images to guide emotion transfer, yet they require high-quality frontal views and face challenges in acquiring reference data for extended emotions (e.g., sarcasm). To address these limitations, we propose Cross-Modal Emotion Transfer (C-MET), a novel approach that generates facial expressions based on speeches by modeling emotion semantic vectors between speech and visual feature spaces. C-MET leverages a large-scale pretrained audio encoder and a disentangled facial expression encoder to learn emotion semantic vectors that represent the difference between two different emotional embeddings across modalities. Extensive experiments on the MEAD and CREMA-D datasets demonstrate that our method improves emotion accuracy by 14% over state-of-the-art methods, while generating expressive talking face videos - even for unseen extended emotions. Code, checkpoint, and demo are available at https://chanhyeok-choi.github.io/C-MET/
affiliations: Ulsan National Institute of Science and Technology (UNIST)

ru.title: Перенос эмоций через модальности: речь управляет мимикой
ru.desc: Статья предлагает новый метод кросс-модального переноса эмоций (C-MET) для генерации выразительных видео говорящего лица на основе речи. Метод моделирует семантические векторы эмоций между пространствами признаков речи и визуальной информации, используя предобученный аудиоэнкодер и развязанный энкодер мимики лица. Подход решает проблему переплетения эмоционального содержания и лингвистической информации в эмоциональной речи, позволяя генерировать эмоции, которые модель не видела во время обучения. Экспериментальные результаты на датасетах MEAD и CREMA-D показывают улучшение точности распознавания эмоций на 14% по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Bridging Speech and Emotion for Realistic Talking Faces
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called Cross-Modal Emotion Transfer (C-MET) that improves the generation of expressive talking face videos by linking speech and visual features through emotion semantic vectors. Unlike previous methods that struggle with emotional flexibility and accuracy, C-MET effectively captures a wider range of emotions by disentangling emotional content from speech. The approach utilizes a pretrained audio encoder and a facial expression encoder to enhance the emotion transfer process, achieving a 14% improvement in emotion accuracy over existing techniques. This advancement allows for the generation of more realistic and expressive videos, even for complex emotions like sarcasm.

zh.title: 跨模态情感转移：生成生动的说话人脸视频
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的跨模态情感转移方法（C-MET），旨在通过建模语音和视觉特征空间之间的情感语义向量，生成富有表现力的说话人脸视频。与现有方法相比，C-MET在情感准确性上提高了14%。该方法利用大规模预训练的音频编码器和解耦的面部表情编码器，学习不同情感嵌入之间的情感语义向量，从而克服了传统方法在情感表达上的局限性。实验结果表明，C-MET能够生成即使是未见过的扩展情感的生动说话人脸视频。
